Hey, welcome to part three of sales mastery unleash the no BS system for closing sales like crazy in this next segment you're gonna learn precisely how to overcome objections and Actually, maybe even learn to love them. That's how powerful this next section is going to be now Nick We're gonna be using a lot from this which is a bonus that comes With this system and but first before we get to the actual scripting of objections. We're gonna do a little role-playing here Damn one of the things that you teach is that you should overcome objections in advance Why most salespeople would say why I don't even want to bring up objections Because I hope the prospect doesn't think of them See the problem the problem with that idea is that really stupid people generally don't have much money So the person who is really so dumb that they are gonna miss the flaws in the ointment Probably can't afford to pay much for the ointment. So I'd rather sell to smart people sure any day of the week so You want to So I think there's three things that are true one is you want to have dealt with At least not have leave it to be a surprise if not have fully erased as many objections as you possibly can in Advance of the actual Sales event Right, so some are erased without people even realizing that's what they're doing Right, so somebody goes to a showroom to buy a Volvo They have their long past the objection that it's an ugly car, okay with a horrible name. They're over that right Joni's to complain about it. So if a Jewish person goes to buy a Volkswagen they are over The object that it's a you know a Nazi car. This has been taken care of sort of automatically But often they're not automatic. They have to be thought through so I Will almost always prefer raising the curtain on the elephant lurking in the room on the theory that He can be smelled and better to paint him purple with yellow polka dots and trot them out and try and tell everybody why It's cool that you get the elephant as a bonus with the product, right? Or acknowledge it is damaging admission, but these 56 benefits far outweigh the fact that you have to wait eight weeks When you really want it tomorrow or that you know or whatever Secondly, it's important to do it Again in any kind of environment where the selling is not being entirely done by you face to face and you are highly skilled Okay, so again, we can wait if we wish and Can deal with every objection if we employ ten people We're going to be in trouble if we employ a hundred. It's going to be hopeless All right, if we sell at a distance then we don't get human interaction, right? So we have to anticipate and answer Let the last reason to do it is because it allows you to use your prime time Which is the face-to-face or on the phone? closing of the sale Having to deal only with two or three instead of eight ten twelve fifteen or twenty sometimes answering objections turns into this ping-pong game and The prospect has a whole pocket full of ping-pong balls, right? So He hits one over to you you hit it off the board He's got another one and this goes on so long nobody even remembers the sales presentation Everybody's just exhausted, you know, it's just not good. So if you can narrow it down to three or four things You know we're going to be difficult that you know you're going to have to deal with but at least you've eliminated Eight ten twelve other ones that I think is is beneficial Obviously sales there are gonna be objections that come up that you may not have possibly they you they You're not handling in advance. I think a lot of sale in fact. I know a lot of sales people This is when they crumble right they get an objection They they either don't know how to answer it they stumble or they actually give up itself Person's not interested address that Nick. Yeah, it happens all the time I've been on enough ride-alongs where you're with somebody and the whole way there They're the best salesperson in the world you sit down with the prospect of prospect throws the first objection They said okay, thanks, and they get we were like whoa Let's slow down a little bit because they think that the objection is really I think it's more just part of the buying process
